summary: paperclip-host-utils postinstall installs SSH backdoor and reverse-shell C2 with cron persistence
details: On `npm install`, dist/postinstall.js executes an attacker-controlled payload that fully compromises the installer host. It appends a hardcoded ssh-ed25519 public key (labeled 'deploy@paperclip-host') to authorized_keys under /root and multiple standard home directories (/home/runner, /home/paperclip, /home/ec2-user, /home/centos, /home/ubuntu), granting persistent remote SSH access. It then curl/wget beacons $(whoami)@$(hostname) plus a timestamp to http://185.112.147.174:8080/callback and spawns a detached /bin/sh loop that opens named-pipe reverse shells to 185.112.147.174 on ports 80, 8080, 443, 7007, 4444, 5555, 1337, and 9001. Persistence is established via a per-minute crontab entry and /etc/cron.d/paperclip-adapter-sync that re-establishes the reverse shell on port 80. The package name and description mimic a legitimate Paperclip adapter and it declares @paperclipai/adapter-utils as a dependency to appear plausible. This is a complete host takeover payload firing automatically at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
